mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi on Kubernetes
mission.description: Strimzi provides a way to run an Apache Kafka cluster on Kubernetes or OpenShift in various deployment configurations. Installing Strimzi allows you to easily manage Kafka clusters and their components within your Kubernetes environment using the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

steps.title: Install Strimzi Kafka Operator via OCI Helm chart
steps.description:
Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator using the OCI Helm chart (preferred method):

```bash
helm install strimzi oci://quay.io/strimzi-helm/strimzi-kafka-operator \
  --namespace kafka \
  --create-namespace
```

**Alternative: Using the traditional Helm repository**
```bash
helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator \
  --namespace kafka \
  --create-namespace
```

To watch all namespaces:
```bash
helm install strimzi oci://quay.io/strimzi-helm/strimzi-kafka-operator \
  --namespace kafka \
  --create-namespace \
  --set watchAnyNamespace=true
```

steps.title: Verify the operator installation
steps.description:
Check that the Strimzi Kafka Operator pod is running:

```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kafka
```

You should see the strimzi-cluster-operator pod in Running state.

steps.title: Deploy a Kafka cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kafka cluster by applying the Strimzi example Kafka resource:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://strimzi.io/examples/latest/kafka/kafka-persistent.yaml --namespace kafka
```

Wait for the Kafka cluster to be ready:

```bash
kubectl wait kafka/my-cluster --for=condition=Ready --timeout=300s -n kafka
```

steps.title: Verify the Kafka cluster
steps.description:
Check that all Kafka cluster pods are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kafka
```

You should see pods for ZooKeeper (or KRaft controllers), Kafka brokers, and the entity operator.

uninstall.title: Remove Kafka clusters
uninstall.description:
Delete any Kafka cluster resources before removing the operator:

```bash
kubectl delete kafka my-cluster --namespace kafka
```

Wait for all Kafka pods to terminate.

uninstall.title: Remove the Strimzi operator
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Strimzi Helm release:

```bash
helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove Strimzi CRDs and the namespace:

```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep strimzi.io | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete namespace kafka
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi operator
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the operator using the OCI chart. Note: CRDs are not upgraded automatically by Helm and must be updated manually:

```bash
helm upgrade strimzi oci://quay.io/strimzi-helm/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka
```

After the operator upgrade, update the Kafka cluster version in the Kafka resource if needed.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the operator and Kafka cluster are running correctly:

```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kafka
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the pod logs for errors:

```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kafka
```

troubleshooting.title: Kafka cluster not responding
troubleshooting.description:
Check the status of the Kafka pods and the Kafka resource:

```bash
kubectl get kafka --namespace kafka
kubectl describe kafka my-cluster --namespace kafka
```

troubleshooting.title: Persistent volume claims not bound
troubleshooting.description:
If PVCs are not bound, check that a StorageClass is available:

```bash
kubectl get pvc --namespace kafka
kubectl get storageclass
```

troubleshooting.title: Operator not reconciling
troubleshooting.description:
Check the operator logs:

```bash
kubectl logs -l strimzi.io/kind=cluster-operator --namespace kafka
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Strimzi Kafka Operator running in the kafka namespace, and you will be able to create and manage Kafka clusters using Kafka custom resources. The operator handles all lifecycle management including scaling, rolling updates, and configuration changes.

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster (v1.25+) with a default StorageClass for persistent Kafka clusters, Helm v3, and kubectl.
